mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals ongoing labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of months with positive PAYEMS growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, restrictive monetary policy raises discount rates and debt service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirm a synchronized expansion in both manufacturing output and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that are stronger than either signal alone.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO and RSAFS both increasing does not produce higher average 20-day returns than the average of the two independent single-series signals over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in housing starts (HOUST) during periods of near-zero federal funds rates signals a construction-led demand recovery amplified by cheap mortgage financing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, positive HOUST changes do not produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the effect is not stronger than in non-ZIRP periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) erode real corporate earnings expectations and pressure the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL surprises do not correlate with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns; Rank IC is greater than zero instead of negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in retail sales (RSAFS) signal weakening consumer demand that directly reduces corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of cases after two consecutive monthly declines in RSAFS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 6.5% signals a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6.5%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal contracting manufacturing activity that precedes broader economic slowdown, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month INDPRO changes do not produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.
